entry.title: Re: problems with ui5 application deployments
entry.content:
It would require an account with access to the backend ABAP system with authorization for the Adaptation Transport Organizer (ATO).

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Get Oldest SFC in Queue At Given Resource (& Queue Ma...
entry.content:
Hi Tobias, thank you for your fast answer. Unfortunately, calling this API is not an option since the amount of SFCs in Queue will be huge.

Regarding MDOs, I don't want to use them in a production process since SAP states that they are not always up to date and should only be used for reports/dashboards.

entry.title: Re: #abap_trial: Application Server time
entry.content: Lots of answers here: https://www.google.com/search?q=%22ZDATE_ILLEGAL_LOCTIME%22%20site:sap.com (search "ZDATE_ILLEGAL_LOCTIME")

entry.title: Re: problems with ui5 application deployments
entry.content: thank you for your reply @Ryan-Crosby . Basically it would be the credentials that I use in SAP LOGON to enter the development environment, right? and another doubt, why I get this if I did not activate that service?

entry.title: Re: Fetch duplicate records from database table
entry.content: What error? Syntax error? "The field "LOGSYS" from the SELECT list is missing in the GROUP BY clause"?

entry.title: Re: HIDE SALES ORDER PRICE FIELDS
entry.content: Hi, Vivek_Savant, I will check the corresponding documentation. Thank you very much

entry.title: Fiori MyPaystub – Ireland vs UK PDF issue
entry.content:
Hi

Trying to get the Fiori MyPaystub app working and have hit the following issue.

UK payslip works fine – both custom payslip and SAP standard SAP_PAYSLIP_GB work

Irish payslip  neither custom or SAP standard work. SAP_PAYSLIP_IE  raises  ‘The PDF file could not be loaded’

 

These are both standard HRFORMS

 

 

Both work fine thru the SAP GUI

I am assuming features EDTIN and EDPDF are not relevant and anyway there is nothing in these specifically for the UK.

Assuming I have the feature HRFOR configured correctly where else should I be looking ?

 

Additional info - turns out my two test users (IE and UK) were setup slightly differently and my issue was authorisation related!

Regards

Brendan

entry.title: Re: problems with ui5 application deployments
entry.content: @MaximoSanchez yes, it would be your SAP logon credentials. I would assume it would only be looking for access to ATO if your project is an adaptation project.

entry.title: Re: Can I write the one or Standard Unit Test for my all ABAP Code ?
entry.content: I don't understand the question. So, you have "already developed Unit Test". What do you want to do exactly? What is "Standardized Unit Test"? Why did you tag "function module" and "debugging"?

entry.title: Re: problems with ui5 application deployments
entry.content: ok @Ryan-Crosby, I will try, thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: For an in-depth look at clean core and how to implement it, read our Clean Core Extensibility pa
entry.content:
In connection with the clean core strategy, you may be interested in this blog post from @thomas_volmering:

How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?

entry.title: Re: ME22N Exit
entry.content:
Hello @Byz1 

Purchase order custom fields are implemented with the ME_GUI_PO_CUST BAdI enhancement.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Building Robust Enterprise Architecture: Unlocking Value Streams (In Business Context)
entry.content: @JansiMurugesan—This is an excellent blog. Linking business objectives to KPIs via value stream and capabilities makes a lot of sense.

entry.title: Re: Can I write the one or Standard Unit Test for my all ABAP Code ?
entry.content: So the question is: Do I need to write a separate code for Unit Testing each time when I write a new code (for different purpose)? Or does it have any possibility to create the Template or Standard Code for Unit testing (like a Class and method) which we can use do to a Unit testing.

entry.title: Re: Sales order with reference to contract, equal quantity.
entry.content:
Hi Matsikun, 

Thanks for your message.

But, I tested with this option in VOV7 edit than C for B but unsucessefull in this change.

Will have other alternative or parameter for this point ?

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 4
entry.content:
Hi @carlos_vegasgarca 

are you using the Portal service which is documented here instead of the Launchpad Service?
In this scenario you do not create destinations to connect the app-hosts but you have to bind all app-hosts to the Portal content deployer module.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: Re: Clean Core demystified: What does it mean and how to achieve it with SAP BTP?
entry.content:
In connection with the clean core strategy, you may be interested in these blog posts:

From @steffen_pietsch: "For an in-depth look at clean core and how to implement it, read our Clean Core Extensibility paper!"

From @thomas_volmering: "How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?"

entry.title: Re: SAP BAS Adaptation Project - SAPUI5 Visual Editor Error
entry.content:
Nice tip! I was having issues unrelated to this thread, and this solution worked for me. Just wanted to share the problem and solution with others:

Problem: Establishing communication between BTP using Destinations with basic authentication against an S/4Hana Public Cloud instance resulted in the following error being displayed in the server logs:
"... Since your browser does not support JavaScript, you must press the Continue button once to ..."

Solution: Due to IAS settings, even with a technical user defined through the "Communication User" tile, service requests might be redirected to SAML authentication. The parameter URL.headers.x-sap-saml2: disabled did the trick.

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Request (Istanbul, Turkey)
entry.content: Hi Hilal, feel free to contact me directly and we can discuss possible dates 🙂

entry.title: Role Name and Family from Job Profile Builder on Employee Profile/EC/Job Info
entry.content:
Hi all, 

We use Job Profile Builder to manage Job Roles and Families. We use Employee Central where everyone has a job code/job classification, which is mapped to the relevant 'Role' entity:

  • Employee has a job code/job classification
  • Job code/job classification is mapped to a Role
  • The Role has a Family as one of its attributes

Each Role entity has a Role Name and belongs to a Family in JPB. What I'd like to know is, is it possible for this Role Name and Family Name to be mapped back to the employee's job info on their employee profile? Family is the main one we're interested in. We also have several downstream integrations where we would like to include the Family as part of the integration file. So we're just exploring what's in the art of the possible currently, and wanted to see if anyone in the community had done something like this already. 

(Another consideration is whether it's possible for the JPB Role Name and Role Family to be brought through into the Position object once the job code has been populated)

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Details of SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology Phase IV (Data Governance & Roadmaps)
entry.content:
In the previous blogs, I have provided details of the first three phases of the methodology for developing a target architecture for data-driven business outcomes:

Phase I: Scoping & Baseline Analysis
Phase II: Business Outcomes & Solution Requirements
Phase III: Capability Map & Solution Architecture

This blog is about the final phase, with focus on data governance and roadmap planning.

Overview of phase IV

Phase IV concludes your exploration of a new target architecture by assessing your data governance and organizational aspects and creating a roadmap or project plan for implementing the new architecture.

In the first two steps of this phase, you investigate relevant data governance and organizational aspects by applying the “Data and Analytics Maturity Assessment”. This topic is important as it is a key enabler to successfully establish the chosen architecture und realize the business outcomes defined in phase II.

Step 3 of this phase describes how to realize the data & analytics solution architecture by consolidating and planning all required activities and creating a high-level roadmap or project plan.

Steps IV.1 & 2: Data Governance / Roles & Organization

I have already discussed the details of the “Data and Analytics Maturity Assessment” in the previous blog. It is used to assess the maturity of data governance (step IV.1) and organizational aspects (step IV.2) and to derive the necessary improvement actions from the gaps between the current and desired maturity levels.

The activities identified during the assessment are consolidated and made available for the final step, roadmap planning.

Here is an example:

Step IV.3: Roadmaps/Project Plan

A roadmap is a strategic planning tool designed to align short-term and long-term goals with specific IT solutions. It presents these plans in formats, such as a sunray diagram or a planning chart.

An architecture roadmap organizes activities based on business needs and priorities to achieve the target architecture within a defined time frame. These activities are grouped into functional clusters to streamline planning and execution.

You start by collecting the activities to realize the defined solution architecture in an activity catalog. Each activity is defined with a name, description, and owner. Next, you review the recommended actions from the Data & Analytics Maturity Assessment, break them down into more detailed activities if necessary and add them into the activity catalog.

Then, you organize the activities according to logical clusters. You can define clusters by grouping activities that belong to the same context.

Examples of such clusters include:

  • Architecture Validation (PoC)
  • Implementation
  • Operations
  • Security
  • Know-How Transfer
  • Organization and Governance

Next, you order the activities and identify dependencies to other activities. Finally, you estimate a high-level timeline.

Here is an example of an activity catalogue:

The roadmap is then created based on the activity catalog. The following example shows the roadmap in a sunray diagram.

This completes the Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology. The next step could be the preparation of an implementation project / program where the roadmap is thoroughly planned, resources are assigned, and the architecture design is detailed out.

This article concludes the blog series that introduced the methodology and detailed the phases and steps. But this is not the end,…

…as the methodology is now available in the SAP Help Portal. It provides a comprehensive guidance on how to apply the methodology and includes the content and templates that are provided in the SAP Workzone.

We appreciate any feedback on the methodology and its documentation. We are especially interested in hearing about your experiences using the methodology. Please contact us by e-mail at sap-data-analytics-methodology@sap.com.

entry.title: Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
As most of you know, Joule is now supported on SAP Mobile Start (you can find the release update here). We have had multiple requests to understand how to begin with SAP Mobile Start and what is required to configure it. While this is an excellent opportunity to use Joule with your mobile device, it is also essential to know the released capabilities. This blog will focus on the activation process and the additional information required.

****************************************************************************

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation:

  • SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule Supported Data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc.)
  • Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc.) 
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
  • Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain
    • Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide
  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide

Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start - You are Here

Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP):

  • SAP SuccessFactors Joule Activation (WIP)
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP)
    • SAP Asset Performance Management
    • SAP Product Lifecycle Management
    • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Signavio
  • SAP Concur
  • SAP Ariba
  • and more to be added towards General Availability

****************************************************************************

To begin with, let us understand the details or the options for using SAP Mobile Start (you can find the official help page here). As you have seen in my previous post, we have demonstrated the SAP Build Work Zone standard edition with a foundation plan, an always free service offering basic functionalities. To work with SAP Mobile Start, especially for the Foundation Plan of SAP Build Work Zone, we need additional content known as / that acts as a predefined central entry point and can display role-based content from SAP SuccessFactors,  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, etc.

Things to remember to plan activation of SAP Mobile Start:

  • SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition offers – a “standard plan” and “foundation plan.” You can use SAP Start can be set up with both versions
    • Foundation Plan
      • With the foundation plan, SAP Start is available to all SAP Customers at no extra cost for up to 1,000 active users per month
      • In case more user license is required, please talk to your BTP CSP or Account Executive and look up the details related to service plans and metering
      • SAP Start is offered in limited Data Center Availability locations, and please check the list here before you create your subaccounts
      • If you plan to use SAP Start as a Central Entry Point, you may also be familiar with general restrictions related to SAP Start can be found here
    • Standard Plan
      • It offers full access to the Work Zone Design Time / Site Manager, enabling customers to customize the Sites, Content, Pages, etc., and create custom Sites with all the content. In addition, more content can be added/exposed. Additionally, with Joule, we see visibility on Roles assigned to users.
      • While the Standard Plan is pretty straightforward, I will first demonstrate the Foundation Plan options to set up Joule with the SAP Start for Mobile scenario and then look at the Standard Plan options towards the end.  
      • Offers customization of Sites and Content as well as the creation of new sites” would be more fitting
      • More information on this plan can be found on the help page here

While the Standard Plan is pretty straightforward, I will first demonstrate the Foundation Plan options to set up Joule with the SAP Start for Mobile scenario and then look at the Standard Plan options towards the end.

Another important aspect is to know what use cases are currently supported using SAP Mobile Start. You can refer to the Joule Capabilities help page.

Let us understand the setup process and let us take a look at the steps involved:

a. SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition – foundation plan

  1. Pre-requisites
  2. Manual Setup of SAP Start Formation and validate the Sites
  3. Add Joule to the SAP Start Formation
  4. Test Joule in the SAP Start Site
b. SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition – standard plan(skip it, if you followed the foundation plan setup)
c. Install SAP Mobile Start
d. Login to SAP Mobile Start
 

Let us get started:

a. SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition – foundation plan

1. Pre-requisites:

  • While executing the booster, you have selected SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition in screen 3 (Select Integration) and screen 4 (Select Capabilities)
  • In case you have not done this, you can go to SAP Business Technology Platform cockpit, expand the Systems options, click on Formations, search for the formation name, and include SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition subscription activated in the subaccount to the formation

Before we get started, please check the formation you have included in the SAP Build Work Zone listed below. If it's not listed, you can click on Include Systems, select your SAP Build Work Zone, and add it. 

Image 1

 2. Manual Setup of SAP Start Formation and validate the Sites

Let me start with a background: SAP BTP provides a Booster to activate the SAP Start; however, this is only applicable when creating a new subaccount. We are going to activate our requirement using the manual approach.  

In your SAP BTP Cockpit, navigate to System Landscape -> click on Formations -> click on Create Formation. 

For documentation, I am using the example of a subaccount with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, as shown below.

Image 2

In the Create Formation page, enter the Formation Name (ideally a similar name to your Joule subaccount where you are activating SAP Start), select the Formation Type – Integration with SAP Start as shown below, and click on Next Step.

Image 3

In the next screen, filter the “All System Types” options to SAP Build Work Zone, select the subaccount where you would like to activate the service and click on Next Step.

Image 4

On the final screen, review the settings and click on Create.

Image 5

Once you complete it, you should be able to see the formation in the Ready state, as shown below.

Image 6

To test this activation, navigate to your Joule subaccount, click on Instances and Subscriptions, and launch your SAP Build Work Zone. You should be able to see the SAP Start.

Image 7

This confirms your activation of SAP Start in your subaccount.

3. Add Joule to the SAP Start Formation

We need to add Joule to the SAP Start Formation we created in Step 2, which will allow the Joule capabilities to be integrated with the SAP Start site. To do this, go to your SAP BTP Global Account, click on System, click on Formations, look for the SAP Start Formation you just created, and click on Include Systems.

Image 8

In the “All System Types,” filter the options to Joule, select your Joule Subaccount, and click on Next Step.

Image 9

Before you add this, verify the details and click on Include.

Image 10

You should now be able to see the formation with the Joule service included.

Image 11

Once the status is “Ready”, we should be able to see the Joule Icon in the SAP Start Site.

4. Test Joule in the SAP Start Site

Navigate to your SAP Build Work Zone and launch your SAP Start Site. You should be able to see the Joule icon, as shown below.

Image 12

Click on the Joule icon to test it.

Note: I have demonstrated a stand-alone SAP Start system, and no integration to SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has been established at this time.

b. SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition – standard plan (skip it, if you followed the foundation plan setup)

This process applies if you have activated the SAP Build Work Zone, standard plan with Joule, as shown in Image 1. To activate Joule, you need a Site. If your SAP Build Work Zone is new and without any sites, you can navigate to the Site Directory and click on Create Site.

Image 13

Give a Site Name and click on Create. Once the site is created, click on settings:

Image 14

Click on Edit and look for Joule under the Service option. Turn it on as shown below, and save the settings.

Image 15

If you Navigate to the Mobile Test site, you should be able to see the Joule icon, which is connected to the LoB systems that you have configured as per your requirements. The image below shows the service up and running.

Image 16

c. Install SAP Mobile Start

You can install SAP Mobile Start directly via the App Store or Google Store, or within your SAP Site, you can click on your profile, select settings, and click on the SAP Mobile Start Application. Here, you will be able to select either iOS or Android and select Install to scan the QR Code.

d. Login to SAP Mobile Start

Once you install the SAP Mobile Start application, launch it, select “Register” from the SAP Start page, and enter your login details.

Image 17

To use “SAP Mobile Start,” click on your profile icon next to Joule, then click on Settings -> click on SAP Mobile Start Application.

This completes the setup of Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and using Joule with SAP Mobile Start.

Image 18

If you have any questions about the setup, you can email us at SAP_AI_RIG@sap.com with the subject line "Joule—SAP Mobile Start." 

Happy Learning!!!

Regards,

Nagesh Caparthy

entry.title: Porque la MD04 toma las necesidades primarias de VENTAS de la MC88? Necesito que tome las de Producc
entry.content:
Estimados, estoy tendiendo un problema  que hace rato no puedo solucionar!
A través de un Programa Z le cargamos actualizamos la tabla S076 cargándole el forecast de ventas, automáticamente aparecen los datos en la MC88. Ventas, Produccion y cobertura prevista. El problema esta en que cuando corro los procesos MC8E y MC8G se tendrian que transferir los datos de produccion como necesidades primarias en la MD04, lo que me esta pasando es que en vez de tener los datos de produccion como Nesecidad me aparecen los datos de ventas.
Alguien sabe como puedo hacer para que me aparezcan los datos de produccion como necesidad?

Saludos

entry.title: Get the contracts list whose value is greater than 1 million & between 500k to 1 Mil Using Radio But
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I have a Requirement, Where user wants to get the list of Orders whose value is greater than 1 Million, 500k-1Million, 100K-500K and <100K using a Radio button. Below is the sample table data. I have introduced a Radio button as below.

What I'm not sure is that how could I achieve this using Java Script in SAC.

Regards,

Ram

entry.title: Problem Doing ODATA Request with XCSRF Token
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

I want to call an ODATA Endpoint of my RAP Service in my On Premise System, which is exposed via Cloud Connector in BTP
First, I have to fetch the 'x-csrf-token' via axios.get. Therefore, I encoded my username and password and add it to basic authentication. I receive a token from the response headers. With that token in the header I request my Entity, but after that I always get an error:  401 Not authorized

 

 

const axios = require("axios");

const sTokenUrl = "https://MYSYSTEMURL:MYPORT/sap/opu/odata/sap/MYSERVICE";
const sBase64UserNamePassword = "USERNAME:PASSWORD"; // This is Base64Encoded
const sServiceUrl = "https://MYSYSTEMURL:MYPORT/sap/opu/odata/sap/MYSERVICE/MYENTITY"

const oTokenResponse = await axios.get(sTokenUrl, {
            headers: {
                "x-csrf-token": "fetch",
                Authorization: `Basic ${sBase64UserNamePassword}`,
                Accept: "*/*",
                "Cache-Control": "no-cache",
                "Accept-Encoding": "gzip, deflate, br",
                Connection: "keep-alive",
            },
            withCredentials: true,
        });

const aCookies = oTokenResponse.headers["set-cookie"];
const sAuthToken = oTokenResponse.headers["x-csrf-token"];
const oHeaders = {
   "X-Csrf-Token": sAuthToken, 
   Accept: req.headers.accept,
   "Content-Type": req.headers["content-type"] ?? "application/json",
   "Cookie": aCookies.join(";")
};
try {
   const response = await axios.get(sServiceUrl, { headers: oHeaders });
} catch (oError) {
    console.log("Request Error:", oError.message);                   
}

 

 

If I do the same thing with Postman, it works. Can anybody help me with that ? 

Thanks
Daniel

entry.title: Re: Can I write the one or Standard Unit Test for my all ABAP Code ?
entry.content: The purpose of unit test is to have your code run for each possible scenario. Thus this requires carefully writing the testable code. Please read about this topic here https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/acquire-core-abap-skills/implementing-code-tests-with-abap-unit_b23c7a00-c2e8-406d-8969-b00db3f1fd87

entry.title: SAC Planning
entry.content:
Planning Data entered at higher node should distribute as per percentage of Actuals.

 

Ex: . When data entered in planning table(for Feb 2025) at  higher node it should split according to the percentage of actual value of (feb 2024).

entry.title: How i create a BR to change AddressOrigin
entry.content:

i have problem with the AddressOrigin in the activity section, so when i change an equipment, the addressOrigin change to User defined and the address in the Mobile app not updated.

i tried to create a BR help me to fix the address origin to service Call Equipment when i change the equipment

this is the address and the addressOrigin when i create an activity with the first equipment:

 

 

And this is what happens when I change the equipment for the same activity:

 

from here i change the equipment :

and this is the BR that i tried to create: 

 

hope you can help me 

Thank you

My question is is you can do central procurement with the BP J45.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partner
entry.content:
Hi @Rodrigo_Kamenach, regarding the use of PUT and DELETE, you can only change something that you previously created. And if it was created correctly, check the ID as per the instructions in SAP Business Accelerator Hub. If the problem persists, please provide more details about the steps you performed. Thank you!

https://api.sap.com/api/pdf_service/overview

entry.title: Hello everyone
entry.content:
Hello, My name is Manabu Inoue. I'm an architect. I have a long experience in scratch development and is familiar with CAP (Java), SAPUI5, and HANA Cloud among SAP products. I am also interested in S/4HANA extension development.

Previously, I translated Clean ABAP into Japanese https://github.com/SAP/styleguides/blob/main/clean-abap/CleanABAP_ja.md and it was introduced on https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/clean-abap-translations-and-german-version-of-the-book/ba-p/13543990.

I look forward to sharing various knowledge with you all.

entry.title: Re: SAP SPM Incentive Management - Advanced Workflow White Paper - Call Workflow REST APIs in GCP
entry.content: Nice explanation, Raul! This really helped me connect to the workflow REST APIs.

entry.title: Launching the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard for Your SAP Transformation Journey
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, transformation is essential. As customers embark on their journey, they require tools that provide clarity on their current position, the status of key projects, and insights that guide their next steps and strategy. This is where the RISE with SAP dashboard proves invaluable, helping organizations keep their ERP systems agile, innovative, and optimized for continuous improvement, unlocking SAP’s full potential.

What is the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard?

The RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard is designed to support businesses in their move towards a clean core approach. This approach focuses on building resilient business processes and extensions supported by seamless integration, efficient operations, and data quality. It emphasizes the adoption of standard processes and the creation of extensions for differentiation, resulting in streamlined operations and increased efficiency.

Clean core is essential to help ensure customers’ business-critical systems remain agile, cost-effective, and ready to adopt innovations that drive business forward. The RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard is designed to support customers on their transformation journey to a clean core landscape.

Key Features and Benefits

The dashboard delivers a comprehensive set of capabilities to monitor and track the status of your systems, offering valuable insights and recommendations and ensuring alignment with SAP's best practices.

Designed for lead architects, the first release of the dashboard focused on clean core dimensions and provides the following features:

  • Access a detailed overview of system adherence and optimization opportunities for production or non-production S/4HANA, directly aligned with SAP's clean core approach
  • Analyze detailed insights and data on the clean core status of your systems, hosted on the SAP Cloud ALM (Application Lifecycle Management). SAP Cloud ALM is a key component of our integrated toolchain, offering a robust cloud environment optimized for daily updates and frequent functionality deployments.
  • Manage system extensibility with the dashboard, to identify deviations from SAP's recommendations and prepare for future upgrades. It supports maintaining a streamlined system with minimal customizations. This ensures that your systems are ready for future upgrades and that any necessary changes are well planned and executed.

                        System View to review your current extensions

 

We are committed to continuously enhancing its features and add new persona-based views, with regular updates that introduce new capabilities for clean core dimensions and transformation accelerators.

Next Steps: How to Get Started

The RISE with SAP is available to all customers with a RISE contract. Starting today customers can now access the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard.

Want to learn more about how you can leverage this powerful tool for your SAP S/4HANA systems? Visit the SAP Help Portal to access detailed resources on the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard. There, you’ll find step-by-step guides, FAQs, and best practices to help you get the most out of this innovative tool.

Let’s transform your SAP S/4HANA environment for success!

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partner
entry.content:
Bom dia! Poderia, por favor, nos informar se este desenvolvimento substitui formulário existente para impressão e re-impressão do DANFE? Ou seja, quando eu fizer um output pela J1B3N vai buscar também deste modelo criado pelo Adobe LiveCycle Designer?

grato,

Nilson

entry.title: Hi all
entry.content:
Hi guys,


thanks for having me. I'm happy to be part of this group.
Its great to have a place to share ideas and learn new things.

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

hope you can help out here. We were moving payment methods via CTC, however the payment method assignments were not updated. Is this expected? The import was successfull according to CTC.

Thanks,
Linda

entry.title: Release Blog: Retail, Fashion and Vertical Business in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content: Retail, Fashion and Vertical Business in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502

entry.title: Manage payload coming from an custom Open connector's webhook
entry.content: I created a custom Open connector and configured a webhook. In the payload, there is data related to an order created from our application. What are the possibilities to be able to create the order to a HANA for example? Is it mandatory to create an Integration Flow to make that happen or is there other solutions?

entry.title: Work Schedule Upload
entry.content:
Hello All

We are trying to upload work schedules for employees (Master data upload) using the template (object work schedule) from import and export data.

The error is '’Template contains more than one association.’’

 Please let us know if the template selection is correct or if any modification is required.

 

Reg

SK

entry.title: Problems using Eclipse 2024-06 (4.32.0) Build id: 20240606-1231
entry.content:
Hi, I am faced with the problem in Eclipse that in the ABAP perspective the attached "SAP HANA System Libary", folder "Repository content" could not fetch data. While expanding the folder it flickers and in the status bar is it switching between "Fetching children of Repository content (5%)...." and "Selecting node...." continualy. But without any result. I already detached the libary and attached it again. No change in behaviour. 

And my SAP message was "refused" refering to this:

Could someone of you help?

Regards,

Peter

entry.title: Re: Work Schedule Upload
entry.content:
Hi

see https://me.sap.com/notes/2315354/E

Brendan

entry.title: Are you an administrator and need an introduction to configuration tools of Concur Expense Standard?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Expense Standard for Administrators learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is an introduction to Concur Expense Standard for Administrators and Functional Consultants. In this comprehensive journey, you will learn the configuration tools and components of Concur Expense Standard, from the Basic and Core Configuration, to Payments, and finally the optional configurations.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, administrators and functional consultants will be able to,  in Concur Expense Standard:

  • Navigate the Expense Settings page
  • Configure the tools and components
  • Process payments and extracts
  • Navigate reporting 

Prerequisites
Learners should have a firm understanding of how Concur Expense operates as a business user.
Please complete the Getting Started with Concur Expense for Customer Users learning journey before completing this course. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Can we configure the default to allow users to remove...
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you ever get an answer to this question? Trying to find the same answer today... Crossing fingers the option is there.

Thanks,

Heather

entry.title: Re: How to define custom geographic areas in a SAP Analyt...
entry.content:
Hello chmbilal, Sorry for replying late.

I edited my question adding more info.

Thanks a lot for your time!

entry.title: Re: Work Schedule Upload
entry.content: Hello BGibbons

entry.title: Re: Work Schedule Upload
entry.content: Hello Brenden, We checked the page it is for workschedule config uploads (there is no user ID or employee number field available). what we are looking is for employee master data upload for work schedcule

entry.title: S4H Fiori Launchpad Message - Password login no longer possible - too many error attempts!
entry.content:
Dear all, 

We are currently experiencing an issue with the message displayed in the Fiori Login Launchpad. If users enter their password incorrectly too many times, the SAP default message: "Password login no longer possible - too many failed attempts" should be displayed. However, in the S4H environment, this message is not displayed. Even though the user is locked in the backend system. Please have a look at the attached screenshot (we use an embedded architecture and the authentication is done in the backend system). In a non-S4H environment, the message is displayed correctly.

I have searched the SAP Knowledge Base for any notes but could not find anything related to this issue.

Is there a parameter that we need to adjust/change?

Thank you in advance. 

Best regards

Dan

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 4
entry.content:
Hi @matthias_schmalz,

I'm not using the Portal service or any other service like Work Zone. My intention is for each application to work independently; however, I need to reuse some logic and UI components. My question is: without a managed approuter, is it possible to connect each application to the library?
Regards
Carlos

entry.title: Re: SAP Tech Bytes: UI Theme Designer - Applying Custom Theme to UI5 Application
entry.content: Hi @aditi01 , does your exported directory contain the SAP-icons font face somewhere?

entry.title: Re: Attach File with CBO in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Ali_iftikhar

I believe this is not supported to make attachments to the CBO itself.

CBOs are not listed as supporting HDM (https://me.sap.com/notes/3213661). Checking the SSCUI, it is not an option to activate them for HDM (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/bc275e43f1b043cc8f97710d5de64e97.html?locale=en-US).

Looking at Document Management (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7489fa08cede494cbdf08fa3651598af/64b4f4c0d32e49c4a741ea056bef91c7.html?locale=en-US), I don't see a way to do it.

Maybe look into a Document Info Record?

If you are developing a side-by-side application in BTP for example, you could support this.

Developer Extensibility might also be an option.

As I mentioned, you could consider hosting the attachment elsewhere and provide a URL to that file and put the URL in the CBO.

entry.title: Re: Project Settlement in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
concerning currency conversion:

  • with parallel currencies active (which is always the case in public cloud edition) we do not convert currencies within settlement, we are just "moving" the values currency by currency
  • if documents with different currencies are posted to a object that is settled afterwards, we will keep the different transactions currencies for the settlement postings,e.g.
    • postings to Project A:
      transaction currency:        100 EUR, 200 USD, 300 GBP
      company code currency:  670 EUR (100 + 210 + 360)
    • settlement (50% cctr 1, 50% cctr. 2)
    • postings to cctr 1
      transaction currency:        50 EUR, 100 USD, 150 GBP
      company code currency:  335 EUR

entry.title: Re: OData V4 Annotation URI For SWFVISU
entry.content: @MioYasutake -- is this something that you can help with? 😊

entry.title: MASS Change Scheduling Agreement Schedule: Delivery Schedule for Item
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

My client has a requirement of MASS updating Delivery Schedule for Scheduling Agreement Lines.

Similar to the process of accessing ME38, selecting an item and going to Delivery Schedule button. But they want to do this massively for more than one Agreement at a time.

I tried using MASS and BUS2013, but I see the only tables available to mass change are EKKO and EKPO. If I'm not mistaken, the table that stores the Delivery Schedule data is EKET. 

They want to be able to change Item Delivery Date (EKET-EINDT), Item Scheduled Quantity (EKET-MENGE) and so on.

I know SAP doesn't recommend to change the Business Object and add Table/Fields. Is it possible to perform this requirement in a standard way?

entry.title: Re: FTP over SSH in ABAP using SAPFTP
entry.content:
Just to clarify some things for those who will find this topic as me.
SSL Tunnel creates the portforwarding from the SAP Application Server to the SFTP Server. It should work like this:
1. Command "ssh -L 21:sftpserver:22 localhost" creates the SSH tunnel between SAP AppServer and SFTP Server. This requires credentials or SSH Keys to establish the connection. (refer SSH Docs for that).
2. ABAP FTP_CONNECT should connect to the localhost:21
3. SSH Tunnel will forward ABAP request to the port 22 on the SFTP server via secure tunnel.
4. SFTP Server receives this request and responds through the SSH tunnel back to the ABAP FTP_CONNECT.

FTP_CONNECT - is a FM which is implemented in "sapftp" programm. sapftp programm implements only FTP protocol (not SFTP and not FTPS). This is why we should use "external" SSH tunnel over FTP.

entry.title: Re: How to get access to DEV environment login details wi...
entry.content:
Hello,

If you have no access credentials to the instance, you can work on creating a new super admin user directly from Provisioning.

Kindly refer to KBA 2186617 - Creating a Super Admin User in SuccessFactors Provisioning - SAP for Me for guidance. 

You can reach out to your implementation partner with provisioning access to do so. In case you have no partner, you can reach our to customer support requesting that following below steps:

  1. Submit a support case under component LOD-SF-PLT-PRVR.
  2. Include the required details:
    • E-mail address where the credentials will be sent to:
    • Company ID:
    • Data Center where the new user will be created:
    • Reason why a new admin user needs to be created:
  3. Product Support will then create a Super Admin user via Provisioning using the same steps as provided for Partners/Consultants.

entry.title: Scope item's which are available only during initial scoping in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Which are the scope item's in CBC available only during the initial scoping phase of a CBC project in Public Cloud?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Scope item's which are available only during initial...
entry.content:
The list of scope item's available only during initial scoping phase for 2502 are - 1GA, 2VA, 5W2, 5HG, 5KF, 6VN.

To check this in the SAP for Me under process navigator, please follow the steps mentioned in the KBA - 3425018 - CBC: How to check whether a scope item is only available for initial scoping - SAP for Me.

entry.title: Re: Project Settlement in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Birgit,

         thank you for the prompt and clear answer.

Best Regards

John

entry.title: Re: Denmark: Executive order on requirements for the comp...
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP have not delivered any specific new solution related to accounting documents numbering, for Denmark in connection with the new Danish Bookkeeping Act. We believe our products already complies with the requirement for having a “consecutive transaction number or ID” for each individual recorded transaction.

The key requirements are that each transaction should have a unique, sequential identifier, but our understanding there's no explicit demand for a single, common number range for all document types. Here's why:

1) In the Executive order for non-registered systems, like SAP’s products, it says in Section 3, Subsection 2:

Executive order on requirements for the company’s digital bookkeeping systems that aren’t registered according to the Danish Bookkeeping Act

The company must ensure that its digital bookkeeping system adds the following information for each individual recorded transaction: 2) Consecutive transaction number or ID.

This can be interpreted as requiring sequential and unique identifiers for transactions, but it does not explicitly demand a single, shared number range for all document types.

2) In the guidelines for non-registered systems, where accounting document number is mentioned in section 4 (in Danish):

https://erhvervsstyrelsen.dk/ikke-registrerede-digitale-bogfoeringssystemer#chapter4

Chapter 4 - Bogføringstekniske krav (in Danish)

Bilagsnummer: Et felt skal være tilgængeligt for angivelse af et unikt bilagsnummer for hver transaktion.

(Machine translated to English: - Accounting technical requirements

Document Number: A field must be available to specify a unique document number for each transaction)

This again emphasizes uniqueness, not a common number range.

 

3) In the guidelines for non-registered systems, where accounting document number is mentioned in section 4.1 (in Danish):

 4.1. Krav om oplysninger til bogførte transaktioner

Fortløbende transaktionsnummer eller -id: Der skal tildeles et unikt fortløbende nummer eller identifikator til hver transaktion. Dette nummer skal bidrage til at identificere og spore hver transaktion i systemet.

(Machine translated to English: Information requirement for posted transactions

Sequential transaction number or ID: A unique sequential number or identifier shall be assigned to each transaction. This number is intended to help identify and track each transaction in the system.)

This further clarifies that a "unique sequential number or identifier" is required for each transaction to help track and identify it within the system. This aligns with SAP's practice of using different number ranges for different document types, as long as each range is sequential and unique within its type.

The primary objective of the new Danish Bookkeeping Act is to ensure digital bookkeeping with unique IDs and traceability. SAP's approach of using different number ranges for different document types does not seem to contradict these objectives.

I hope the information above give you more clarity.

Best regards,

Ingjerd Aamodt, Localization Product Manager

Localization

entry.title: SF EC payslip pay period warning message
entry.content:
Hi dear experts,

We are facing an warning message when click on pay statement. Pay statement can be seen without a problem however a warning message pops up we want to get rid of this warning message. Systems are EC and s4 Hana (not ECP)

All advices are welcome. (Please see the attachment)

Warning message is as it shown below; 

""Warning

We are having trouble displaying your pay periods. Please contact your system administrator and provide the details below:
Error Code: 001
Error Message: undefined
Root Cause: undefined""

entry.title: How do I submit improvement proposals?
entry.content:
Customers and partners can use the following SAP Continuous Influence Session to submit ideas for improvements for the configuration and pricing services on SAP BTP:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3838

Please review the improvement requests that have already been submitted and vote on them to let us know what is relevant for you.

For more information, see the influence page linked above.

entry.title: Create and manage administrative credentials for accessing the SAP Customer Data Platform API
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Developing with SAP Customer Data Platform learning journey.

Overview
Upon completion of this course, you will be able to successfully create and manage administrative credentials for accessing the SAP Customer Data Platform API, construct code to utilize the Query and Ingestion APIs, perform Event Metadata configuration, set up an Event Listener, and program and configure Events to enrich Customer Data.

Learning objectives
In this course you will be able to:

  • Create and manage administrative credentials required for accessing the SAP Customer Data Platform (CDP) API
  • Construct code on how to utilize the Query API to effectively search and retrieve customer data
  • Develop code on how to use the Ingestion API to add and update customer data within the CDP
  • Perform Event Metadata configuration using the deduplication ID and partitioning event data with the timestamp field
  • Set up an Event Listener to Ingest Customer Data
  • Program and configure an Event to enrich Customer Data using Extensions

 

Prerequisites

  • Exploring SAP Customer Data Platform Essentials
  • Administrating SAP Customer Data Platform

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Equipment fields not visible in display mode, but in edit mode.
entry.content:
Dear all,

In equipment management we have a strange case where some fields are not visible in display mode (IE03)  but in edit mode (IE02).

I put some screen shots.

Do you have any idea ?

Regards

Brice

entry.title: Re: What are the parameters of Function Module
entry.content:
IMPORTING

EXPORTING

CHANGING

TABLES

EXCEPTIONS

entry.title: Smarttable fields are changeable although they have been defined as not changeable
entry.content:
Hello, 

in my SmartTable (UI5) i set my EntitySet which comes from Rap Backend. Here the fields are set as read only. When i bind this fields to smartField it works as expected. The Fields are read only. But in the Smarttable they are editable. 

Code for SmartTable: 

<smartTable:SmartTable
  id="secondSectionTable"
  useVariantManagement="true"
  useTablePersonalisation="true"
  showVariantManagement="true"
  useColumnLabelsAsTooltips="true"
  entitySet="myEntitySet"
  tableType="Table"  
  app:useSmartToggle="true"
  editable="true"
  enableAutoColumnWidth="true"
   showRowCount="true"
  enableAutoBinding="false">

entry.title: Re: smart form print
entry.content:
Hello experts.
In my work, here is my assessment.
The error NORMLY may be related to the ability to execute various processes, records or reports in SAP.

The message does not accurately reflect the source of the error, which is a lack of memory for running processes in SAP.

The suggestion is that you check with your BASIS to alert all users via message that the system is at its limit and some operations may have problems, so they should try again later.

Finally, this is not a customization or development error.

best regards

entry.title: Re: CDS view with Change Data Capture(CDC) for Replication Flow – Part 1
entry.content:
@danielmacho72 I would advice you to look at the restriction mentioned in note 3255746 - Unpermitted usage of ODP Data Replication APIs 
Instead of this oData service, there is now the new "ABAP SQL service" and SAP has in its roadmap the possibility to get data from CDS in delta via this chanel. (see https://www.youtube.com/live/pu_aa-P3d2o )

entry.title: Re: CSP Error in UI API Extension in SAP B1 Webclient
entry.content:
I think this can help: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_ONE_WEB_CLIENT/e6ac71d18c7543828bd4463f77d67ff7/e4e00e09480044de8ae9d87e7090ce64.html?version=10.0_FP_2305&locale=en-US

Go To the Web Client > Settings > General Settings > and there is a text-edit where you can write your site to it

entry.title: Lines with No Open Qty on Scheduling Agreement still appears on ZDSR report
entry.content:
Hello all,

My organization uses the ZDSR100 report to determine what is past due/has not shipped. 

We recently started using scheduling agreements for blanket orders and have been having some issues with items that have already shipped and have no open qty still showing up as past due.

Is there a way to prevent lines with no open qty from showing up on our ZDSR100 report without removing the line from the scheduling agreement? (I've been told that if you remove any lines manually, it could prevent future schedules from posting via EDI. Is this true?)

Thanks everyone in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Re: What You Need to Know About Blogging Post-Migration
entry.content:
Hi @jerryjanda,
Hi fellow bloggers,

is there an overview which HTML is supportend and which isn't?

I have a FAQ blog post where I need to link questions and answers - more or less this is just a simple table of content. How can I do this with the new platform? What I had so far used simple anchors:

<a href="#A1">Question 1</a>

<h3 id="A1">Question 1</h3>
<p>Answer to Question 1</p>

Thanks & Best Regards
Andreas

entry.title: New Pipeline Concept features: integrated runtimes and supporting landscape stages
entry.content:
I like to let you know that a new version 1.0.9 of the Pipeline Concept has been recently shipped with a couple of new enhancements that help you to improve the overall performance, operation and lifecycle when using the Pipelines package for Cloud Integration. At a glance, the following new features and fixes have been introduced:

  • Supporting different tenant stages, e.g., DEV, QA, PRD
  • New integrated messaging runtime "Pipeline Generic Step02 - Integrated Messaging Runtime Async" combining inbound conversion and receiver/interface determination in one pipeline step
  • New integrated messaging runtime "Pipeline Generic Step02 - Integrated Messaging Runtime Sync" for handling synchronous Content-Based-Routing scenarios
  • New template "Pipeline Template Step01 - Inbound Processing Synchronous" for scenario-specific synchronous inbound processing
  • New template "Pipeline Template Step07 - Outbound Processing Synchronous" for scenario-specific synchronous outbound processing
  • Generic inbound processing for XI supports both asynchronous and synchronous messaging
  • Failed test messages end with escalation instead of completed
  • Added custom error handling for generic XI and IDoc inbound flows

The package can be accessed from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. If you have already copied the package to your workspace in your SAP Integration Suite tenant, you can simply run the update of the package to be able to use the latest features.

Let me briefly describe the major new features.

Integrated messaging runtime - Async

In the previous package version 1.0.8, we shipped the option to combine receiver and interface determination in one single XSLT mapping for an improved runtime behavior. Now, with the new version, we introduced a new pipeline step called Pipeline Generic Step02 - Integrated Messaging Runtime Async which combines the inbound conversion as well as the receiver and interface determination in one single pipeline step. This helps to further improve the runtime behavior as well as monitoring and operation.

When using this new integrated pipeline step, you are able to skip most of the other pipeline steps. So, you can reduce the number of JMS queues required to process your messages from 4 to 2 or 8 to 4 when counting in the dead letter queues as well. This integrated messaging runtime only works with the combined receiver and interface determination XSLT, so we can reduce the number of XSLT to a minimum of 1. If you like to use this integrated pipeline instead of the fully decoupled pipeline, you just need to deploy the new generic flow and configure the JMS queue of the scenario-specific inbound processing flow accordingly, by default the name of the inbound processing queue of the integrated pipeline is PIPX01 instead of PIPQ01.

From performance measurements that we did, we know that reading and writing from and to the JMS queues incl. zipping and unzipping the messages as well as fetching the XSLT mappings from the Partner Directory incl. decoding the XSLT require the most CPU consumption within the overall pipeline processing. So, using the integrated messaging runtime helps to reduce the overall CPU consumption compared to the fully decoupled pipeline with 4 generic integration flows instead of 2.

Furthermore, since we run through less number of pipeline steps, the number of message processing logs is also reduced.

We still support the complete pipeline which is shown here:

Recommendation however is to use the integrated pipeline. This picture nicely illustrates which steps and queues can be saved.

Integrated messaging runtime - Sync

With the new version, we now also support a synchronous pipeline. The new pipeline called Pipeline Generic Step02 - Integrated Messaging Runtime Sync combines the receiver and interface determination in one single pipeline step and is suited for handling synchronous Content-Based-Router scenarios. Other than the async pipelines, it doesn't require any JMS queue, instead the pipeline steps are connected using the ProcessDirect adapter which is synchronous by nature.

Supporting landscape stages

On Cloud Integration, you do not necessarily have to have different business system names depending on the landscape stage such as DEV, QA, PRD. For XI and IDoc however you usually do. In SAP Process Orchestration, you maintain the business systems as well as transport groups in its SLD, and when transporting the objects between the stages, the business names are automatically converted. This is not supported in Cloud Integration, so we came up with the following approach to support different stages without the need converting the system names when transporting across the stages.

Within the pipeline, we actually use alias names for the business systems. During runtime, the actual sender business system name is mapped to a sender alias which is used within the pipeline processing, once the receiver alias has been determined, it is then mapped to the actual receiver business name. This allows you to run your scenarios on the different landscape stages without changing the configuration in the Partner Directory such as the XSLT for determining the receivers. So, all partner and scenario definitions in the Partner Directory are the same on all stages based on the alias names. As said, there is no need to do any changes in the XSLT, so this approach is less error prone.

To setup the support for landscape stages, you need to create the business system aliases as partner IDs in the Partner Directory where for each stage you maintain the mapping between alias name and actual names.

Furthermore, to automatically identify the landscape stage of your tenant during runtime, the tenant names are used which are mapped to the corresponding stage IDs. This mapping is stored in the Partner Directory using partner ID SAP_Integration_Suite_Landscape. To do so, you can either use the Partner Directory API or the new Partner Directory user interface.

Single point of entry for any XI inbound scenarios

With the latest enhancements of the Cloud Integration capability of SAP Integration Suite, we support the so called handled by integration flow option for both the XI sender and the XI receiver adapter. In the generic XI inbound processing flow we have changed the XI sender adapter using this very option. With this, we can reduce the number of JMS queues by one. Since we write the messages into an inbound JMS queue anyway, we do not need to use the XI sender specific JMS queue. Furthermore, this option allows us to receive both async and sync calls via the same inbound adapter. So, this reduces the configuration effort in your sender backend, there is no need any more to maintain the IS_URL configuration parameter, all scenarios can be pointed to the very same end point on Cloud Integration.

If you like to try out the pipeline concept in general and the new features in particular, check out this github repository where I describe how to setup sample scenarios using the pipeline concept. Here, we have added the following new scenarios and scenario variants leveraging the new features:

  • Scenario 2D uses the new asynchronous integrated messaging runtime for a recipient list and interface split scenario.
  • Scenario 8 handles a synchronous Point-to-Point with XI inbound.
  • Scenario 9 uses the new synchronous integrated messaging runtime for a synchronous content-based-router pattern.

As usually, in case of feedback, feel free to reach out to me.

entry.title: Is it possible to integrate/link equipment with asset on SAP public cloud?
entry.content: The asset number field does not exist in equipment master so how can we link asset with equipment? Does this functionality exist in standard SAP public cloud system?

entry.title: Re: Project Change Request Feature/APP/Functionality (Por...
entry.content:
Hi Bogdan, 

I understand you are talking about SAP S/4 HANA private cloud, where your are using PPM. 

As per my experience, you can create the different financial plans at ppm item level and then push to PS project under different version in that way you can be able to keep a track of all the changes. 

You can easily compare those versions in SAP PS and then required approvals are processed. 

Hope this helps

Regards 

K

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 4
entry.content:
Hi @carlos_vegasgarca 
in that case you are running the apps standalone. You should provide an html page which contains the needed resource mappings. 
The standalone app router should be able to forward to all apps on the involved app-hosts as well, however the URLs do not contain a destination GUID.

However without more details I won't be able to help you further.

Regards Matthias

entry.title: Re: Disabling the Mobile App for Multiple Profiles
entry.content: Yes, you are correct. This feature can’t be controlled via Admin Center.

entry.title: Re: Upcoming Deprecation of VBScript, its Impact on SAP GUI Scripting and Resulting Strategy
entry.content:
Hello Stefan,

your hint is very useful and interesting, thanks!
By the way, I just tried as you suggested, and created a JS file with exactly your example code in order to just test the first SAP connection mechanism.

I ran it through a CMD session with elevated privileges too, using wscript command followed by filename, but I get the error pop-up below:

 

What could be the reason? is something related to our SAP rules ?
Unfortunately the built-in record & playback scripting feature does not record in JS, cause I wanted to have a sample file from the recording itself, and also in online threads I don't find that much regarding SAPGUI scripting in JS language.

Thanks for the kind answer.

entry.title: Re: How to pass exact error details received from backend...
entry.content: If the backend system sends an error payload, store it in a property. Use a Groovy Script to extract and modify the response and send it as a body to sender.

entry.title: Flexible workflow
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

I have created the Flexible scenario in SWDD_SCENARIO and activated the scenario.

But when I trigger it, I is not triggered. I checked SWEL transaction and there is an error message "Feedback After Error"

Receiver FM SWW_WI_CREATE_VIA_EVE
RFC Destination WORKFLOW_LOCAL_200

 

User Name SAP_WFRT
Main Program SAPMSSY1
Action Feedback after error

 

I have checked RFC Destination WORKFLOW_LOCAL_200 and found that it is working fine. 

Automatic Workflow customizing has been performed in SWU3 transaction.

Maintain Runtime Environment -> Configure RFC Destination.

 

Can somebody help regarding this error?

 

Thanks in advance.

Osman.

 

SAP Business Workflow

entry.title: Re: EKKO-FRGSX not updating for customised Contract Type ZMK
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to update the "Created by" (ERNAM) field in the EKKO table for a contract (Purchasing Document Category (BSTYP) = K). I used 'BAPI_CONTRACT_CHANGE', but it is not updating the "Created by" (ERNAM) field, even though other fields are being updated successfully. I also used the commit BAPI, but the issue persists.

Could you please advise if there is any alternative method to update this field? Are there any available BADIs or User Exits that can be used for this purpose?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Iyappan K

entry.title: Cancellation document pending – Cannot release via VFX3
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am facing an issue with a cancellation document that remains pending and has not generated the expected accounting documents.

Situation:

A billing document was canceled using VF11.
The original invoice belongs to a closed posting period (November), and the cancellation was attempted in December.
The cancellation document remains pending and did not generate the expected accounting entries.

What I tried:

I attempted to release the document using VFX3, but this requires modifying OB52 to reopen the posting period, which is not an option in this case.

Considered Option:

I am evaluating the possibility of canceling the S1 document using an S2 document.
However, I need more details regarding the copy control settings for this process, as I do not have access to SAP Note 388489.

Questions:

Is it viable to cancel S1 with S2 in this scenario?
Could someone provide details about the copy control settings for this process?
Are there alternative solutions to process the cancellation without modifying OB52?


I appreciate any suggestions or experiences you can share.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: CDS view with Change Data Capture(CDC) for Replication Flow – Part 1
entry.content: Thanks, will look into it! I really appreciate the help @Cocquerel !

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

another question: we added 25 country/region specific hris elements to a bundle, however not for all the country code is showing, which is quite confusing. Please see screenshot below. The ones marked red are correct. Do you have any idea how this could be possible and how this should be fixed?

Thanks,
Linda

entry.title: Re: How to check your custom ABAP code for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
@OlgaDolinskaja 

If we have to automate these ATC checks using ABAP AI, how do we enable ? Is there how-to-guide available for customers to review them. Please advise.

landscape: S/4 on-premise 2021 with ISLM available part of 756.

entry.title: Re: SO in SAP B1 - 'Cannot select serial numbers; fractional quantity exists in row 10' error
entry.content: thank you William, I had selected the wrong way of item management. Appreciate it!

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to integrate/link equipment with asset...
entry.content:
Hi Hamza, 

Apparently, this functionality is in the roadmap of SAP (without any specific release date). You can refer the note mentioned below to check in this for the further details 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3196566

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/282355/?section=sectionDetails

Regards 

K

entry.title: Predictive ai with sap ai core
entry.content: Has anyone done the learning predictive ai with sap ai core tutorial on ingesting live data into your house price predictor with sap ai core, someone can help me because I'm stuck at step 15?

entry.title: Hello Community
entry.content:
Hello All,

This is Vedant. I am an EWM consultant with a domain knowledge of Warehousing.

entry.title: Re: IPS Trasformation add Org Member Roles
entry.content:
Hello @dirkwiegele,

I also got the same error message: "An unparsed group id must have exactly 2 or 3 logical parts" but I managed to make it work by creating a new group with the following properties:

  • Name: "aaaaaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-123456789123 organization_user" (pattern: "<org_ID> <org_role>")
  • Display Name: "BTP_CF_PM_*****_OrgUsers" (according to the btp.cf.pm.group.prefix property value in the target system)

Aside from that, I made no changes to the default transformation except removing "%btp.cf.pm.landscape%" from the condition because I didn't want to include the landscape in the Display Name.

From my understanding, the error message is misleading, as it is not the group ID but the group name that must have 2 or 3 logical parts: either "<org_ID> <org_role>" or "<org_ID> <space_ID> <space_role>", as commented in the default transformation.

I hope this helps!

Best regards

Tobias

entry.title: Re: disp-work, Dispatcher, GRAY, Stopped, , ,10102
entry.content: When you added the new system (HANADB SID) you should choose "SAP HANA, platform edition" as the product name, otherwise you won't see the proper license option.
